title: RoboLayout: Differentiable 3D Scene Generation for Embodied Agents
summary: RoboLayout extends layout generation for embodied agents by integrating reachability constraints and agent-aware reasoning into differentiable optimization. The framework supports diverse agent types—from service robots to humans of different capabilities—enabling generation of navigable and actionable indoor scenes. This makes 3D scene generation practical for real-world robotic and interactive applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.05522v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent advances in vision language models (VLMs) have shown strong potential for spatial reasoning and 3D scene layout generation from open-ended language instructions. However, generating layouts that are not only semantically coherent but also feasible for interaction by embodied agents remains challenging, particularly in physically constrained indoor environments. In this paper, RoboLayout is introduced as an extension of LayoutVLM that augments the original framework with agent-aware reasoning and improved optimization stability. RoboLayout integrates explicit reachability constraints into a differentiable layout optimization process, enabling the generation of layouts that are navigable and actionable by embodied agents. Importantly, the agent abstraction is not limited to a specific robot platform and can represent diverse entities with distinct physical capabilities, such as service robots, warehouse robots, humans of different age groups, or animals, allowing environment design to be tailored to the intended agent. In addition, a local refinement stage is proposed that selectively reoptimizes problematic object placements while keeping the remainder of the scene fixed, improving convergence efficiency without increasing global optimization iterations. Overall, RoboLayout preserves the strong semantic alignment and physical plausibility of LayoutVLM while enhancing applicability to agent-centric indoor scene generation, as demonstrated by experimental results across diverse scene configurations.

zh.title: RoboLayout: 用于具身智能体的可微分3D场景生成
zh.summary: RoboLayout通过将可达性约束和智能体感知推理集成到可微分优化中，扩展了具身智能体的布局生成。该框架支持多种智能体类型——从服务机器人到不同能力的人类——实现可导航和可交互的室内场景生成。这使得3D场景生成在现实世界的机器人和交互应用中变得实用。

fr.title: RoboLayout : Génération de scènes 3D différentiables pour agents incarnés
fr.summary: RoboLayout étend la génération de mise en page pour les agents incarnés en intégrant des contraintes d'accessibilité et un raisonnement conscient de l'agent dans l'optimisation différentiable. Le cadre prend en charge divers types d'agents — des robots de service aux humains ayant des capacités différentes — permettant la génération de scènes intérieures navigables et exploitables. Cela rend la génération de scènes 3D pratique pour les applications robotiques et interactives du monde réel.

de.title: RoboLayout: Differenzierbare 3D-Szenengenerierung für verkörperte Agenten
de.summary: RoboLayout erweitert die Layout-Generierung für verkörperte Agenten durch die Integration von Erreichbarkeitsbeschränkungen und agentengerechtem Reasoning in die differenzierbare Optimierung. Das Framework unterstützt diverse Agententypen – von Servicerobottern bis zu Menschen mit unterschiedlichen Fähigkeiten – und ermöglicht die Generierung von navigierbaren und nutzbaren Innenszenen. Dies macht die 3D-Szenengenerierung für reale robotische und interaktive Anwendungen praktisch.

es.title: RoboLayout: Generación de escenas 3D diferenciables para agentes encarnados
es.summary: RoboLayout extiende la generación de diseños para agentes encarnados al integrar restricciones de alcanzabilidad y razonamiento consciente del agente en la optimización diferenciable. El marco admite diversos tipos de agentes—desde robots de servicio hasta humanos con diferentes capacidades—habilitando la generación de escenas interiores navegables y manejables. Esto hace que la generación de escenas 3D sea práctica para aplicaciones robóticas e interactivas del mundo real.

title: Real-Time AI Service Economy: A Framework for Agentic Computing Across the Continuum
summary: This framework analyzes how service-dependency graph topology affects real-time AI services across distributed computing environments, showing that hierarchical dependencies enable stable pricing while complex dependencies cause oscillations. The work proposes hybrid management with cross-domain integrators that reduce price volatility by 70-75% without sacrificing throughput. Dependency structure emerges as a primary determinant of resource allocation reliability in agentic systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.05614v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-time AI services increasingly operate across the device-edge-cloud continuum, where autonomous AI agents generate latency-sensitive workloads, orchestrate multi-stage processing pipelines, and compete for shared resources under policy and governance constraints. This article shows that the structure of service-dependency graphs, modelled as DAGs whose nodes represent compute stages and whose edges encode execution ordering, is a primary determinant of whether decentralised, price-based resource allocation can work reliably at scale. When dependency graphs are hierarchical (tree or series-parallel), prices converge to stable equilibria, optimal allocations can be computed efficiently, and under appropriate mechanism design (with quasilinear utilities and discrete slice items), agents have no incentive to misreport their valuations within each decision epoch. When dependencies are more complex, with cross-cutting ties between pipeline stages, prices oscillate, allocation quality degrades, and the system becomes difficult to manage. To bridge this gap, we propose a hybrid management architecture in which cross-domain integrators encapsulate complex sub-graphs into resource slices that present a simpler, well-structured interface to the rest of the market. A systematic ablation study across six experiments (1,620 runs, 10 seeds each) confirms that (i) dependency-graph topology is a first-order determinant of price stability and scalability,(ii) the hybrid architecture reduces price volatility by up to 70-75% without sacrificing throughput, (iii) governance constraints create quantifiable efficiency-compliance trade-offs that depend jointly on topology and load, and (iv) under truthful bidding the decentralised market matches a centralised value-optimal baseline, confirming that decentralised coordination can replicate centralised allocation quality.

zh.title: 实时AI服务经济：跨越连续体的智能体计算框架
zh.summary: 该框架分析了服务依赖图拓扑如何影响分布式计算环境中的实时AI服务，表明分层依赖关系支持稳定的定价，而复杂依赖关系会导致价格波动。该工作提出了具有跨域集成器的混合管理方法，可将价格波动性降低70-75%，同时不牺牲吞吐量。依赖关系结构成为智能体系统中资源分配可靠性的主要决定因素。

fr.title: Économie des services IA en temps réel : Un cadre pour l'informatique agentique à travers le continuum
fr.summary: Ce cadre analyse comment la topologie du graphe de dépendance des services affecte les services IA en temps réel dans les environnements informatiques distribués, montrant que les dépendances hiérarchiques permettent une tarification stable tandis que les dépendances complexes causent des oscillations. Le travail propose une gestion hybride avec des intégrateurs inter-domaines qui réduisent la volatilité des prix de 70-75% sans sacrifier le débit. La structure de dépendance émerge comme un déterminant principal de la fiabilité de l'allocation des ressources dans les systèmes agentiques.

de.title: Echtzeit-KI-Serviceökonomie: Ein Framework für agentisches Computing im Kontinuum
de.summary: Dieses Framework analysiert, wie die Topologie des Service-Abhängigkeitsgraphen Echtzeit-KI-Dienste in verteilten Rechenumgebungen beeinflusst, und zeigt, dass hierarchische Abhängigkeiten stabile Preisgestaltung ermöglichen, während komplexe Abhängigkeiten zu Schwankungen führen. Die Arbeit schlägt eine hybride Verwaltung mit domänenübergreifenden Integratoren vor, die die Preisvolatilität um 70-75% reduzieren, ohne den Durchsatz zu beeinträchtigen. Die Abhängigkeitsstruktur erweist sich als primärer Bestimmungsfaktor für die Zuverlässigkeit der Ressourcenallokation in agentischen Systemen.

es.title: Economía de servicios de IA en tiempo real: Un marco para computación agentiva a través del continuo
es.summary: Este marco analiza cómo la topología del gráfico de dependencias de servicios afecta los servicios de IA en tiempo real en entornos de computación distribuida, demostrando que las dependencias jerárquicas permiten precios estables mientras que las dependencias complejas causan oscilaciones. El trabajo propone una gestión híbrida con integradores entre dominios que reducen la volatilidad de precios en un 70-75% sin sacrificar el rendimiento. La estructura de dependencia emerge como un determinante principal de la confiabilidad de la asignación de recursos en sistemas agentivos.

title: Reasoning Models Struggle to Control their Chains of Thought
summary: Researchers introduce CoT-Control evaluation showing reasoning models barely control their chain-of-thought reasoning (2.7% for Claude Sonnet 4.5) despite controlling output well (61.9%). This suggests reasoning processes are largely difficult for models to manipulate, which is reassuring for monitoring and interpreting model behavior. The finding suggests CoT monitorability is unlikely to be a failure mode in current systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.05706v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring is a promising tool for detecting misbehaviors and understanding the motivations of modern reasoning models. However, if models can control what they verbalize in their CoT, it could undermine CoT monitorability. To measure this undesirable capability -- CoT controllability -- we introduce the CoT-Control evaluation suite, which includes tasks that require models to solve problems while adhering to CoT instructions, e.g., reasoning about a genetics question without using the word 'chromosome'. We show that reasoning models possess significantly lower CoT controllability than output controllability; for instance, Claude Sonnet 4.5 can control its CoT only 2.7% of the time but 61.9% when controlling its final output. We also find that CoT controllability is higher for larger models and decreases with more RL training, test-time compute, and increased problem difficulty. CoT controllability failures extend even to situations in which models are given incentives (as opposed to direct requests) to evade CoT monitors, although models exhibit slightly higher controllability when they are told they are being monitored. Similarly, eliciting controllability by adversarially optimizing prompts does not meaningfully increase controllability. Our results leave us cautiously optimistic that CoT controllability is currently unlikely to be a failure mode of CoT monitorability. However, the mechanism behind low controllability is not well understood. Given its importance for maintaining CoT monitorability, we recommend that frontier labs track CoT controllability in future models.

zh.title: 推理模型难以控制其思维链
zh.summary: 研究人员介绍了CoT-Control评估，显示推理模型对其思维链推理的控制能力很低（Claude Sonnet 4.5为2.7%），尽管对输出的控制良好（61.9%）。这表明推理过程对模型来说很难操纵，这对监控和解释模型行为是令人欣慰的。该发现表明思维链可监控性在当前系统中不太可能成为失败模式。

fr.title: Les modèles de raisonnement ont du mal à contrôler leur chaîne de pensée
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent l'évaluation CoT-Control montrant que les modèles de raisonnement contrôlent à peine leur raisonnement en chaîne de pensée (2,7% pour Claude Sonnet 4.5) malgré un bon contrôle de la sortie (61,9%). Cela suggère que les processus de raisonnement sont largement difficiles à manipuler pour les modèles, ce qui est rassurant pour le suivi et l'interprétation du comportement des modèles. La découverte suggère que la monitorabilité du CoT est peu probable d'être un mode de défaillance dans les systèmes actuels.

de.title: Reasoning-Modelle haben Schwierigkeiten, ihre Gedankenkette zu kontrollieren
de.summary: Forscher führen die CoT-Control-Evaluierung ein, die zeigt, dass Reasoning-Modelle ihre Gedankenkettenlogik kaum kontrollieren (2,7% für Claude Sonnet 4.5), obwohl sie die Ausgabe gut kontrollieren (61,9%). Dies deutet darauf hin, dass Reasoning-Prozesse für Modelle weitgehend schwer zu manipulieren sind, was für die Überwachung und Interpretation des Modellverhaltens beruhigend ist. Das Ergebnis deutet darauf hin, dass die Überwachbarkeit der Gedankenkette in aktuellen Systemen unwahrscheinlich ein Fehlerfall ist.

es.title: Los modelos de razonamiento luchan por controlar sus cadenas de pensamiento
es.summary: Los investigadores introducen la evaluación CoT-Control que muestra que los modelos de razonamiento apenas controlan su razonamiento de cadena de pensamiento (2,7% para Claude Sonnet 4.5) a pesar de controlar bien la salida (61,9%). Esto sugiere que los procesos de razonamiento son en gran medida difíciles de manipular para los modelos, lo cual es tranquilizador para monitorear e interpretar el comportamiento del modelo. El hallazgo sugiere que la monitorizabilidad de CoT es poco probable que sea un modo de fallo en los sistemas actuales.

title: Evolving Medical Imaging Agents via Experience-driven Self-skill Discovery
summary: MACRO is a self-evolving medical agent that autonomously discovers and synthesizes reusable tool sequences from execution experience, eliminating the need for manual redesign when environments shift. The system uses visual-clinical memory and reinforcement learning to improve multi-step medical imaging analysis. Experiments across diverse imaging tasks demonstrate consistent improvements in orchestration accuracy and cross-domain generalization.
content:
arXiv:2603.05860v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Clinical image interpretation is inherently multi-step and tool-centric: clinicians iteratively combine visual evidence with patient context, quantify findings, and refine their decisions through a sequence of specialized procedures. While LLM-based agents promise to orchestrate such heterogeneous medical tools, existing systems treat tool sets and invocation strategies as static after deployment. This design is brittle under real-world domain shifts, across tasks, and evolving diagnostic requirements, where predefined tool chains frequently degrade and demand costly manual re-design. We propose MACRO, a self-evolving, experience-augmented medical agent that shifts from static tool composition to experience-driven tool discovery. From verified execution trajectories, the agent autonomously identifies recurring effective multi-step tool sequences, synthesizes them into reusable composite tools, and registers these as new high-level primitives that continuously expand its behavioral repertoire. A lightweight image-feature memory grounds tool selection in a visual-clinical context, while a GRPO-like training loop reinforces reliable invocation of discovered composites, enabling closed-loop self-improvement with minimal supervision. Extensive experiments across diverse medical imaging datasets and tasks demonstrate that autonomous composite tool discovery consistently improves multi-step orchestration accuracy and cross-domain generalization over strong baselines and recent state-of-the-art agentic methods, bridging the gap between brittle static tool use and adaptive, context-aware clinical AI assistance. Code will be available upon acceptance.

zh.title: 通过经验驱动的自我技能发现演进医学影像代理
zh.summary: MACRO是一个自我演进的医学代理，它自主发现和综合来自执行经验的可重用工具序列，消除了环境变化时的手动重新设计需要。该系统使用视觉-临床记忆和强化学习来改进多步医学影像分析。在各种成像任务中的实验表明编排准确性和跨域泛化的一致改进。

fr.title: Évolution des agents d'imagerie médicale par découverte autonome de compétences basée sur l'expérience
fr.summary: MACRO est un agent médical auto-évolutif qui découvre et synthétise de manière autonome les séquences d'outils réutilisables à partir de l'expérience d'exécution, éliminant le besoin de redéfinition manuelle en cas de changement d'environnement. Le système utilise la mémoire visuelle-clinique et l'apprentissage par renforcement pour améliorer l'analyse d'imagerie médicale multi-étapes. Les expériences sur diverses tâches d'imagerie démontrent des améliorations constantes dans la précision d'orchestration et la généralisation inter-domaines.

de.title: Weiterentwicklung medizinischer Bildgebungsagenten durch erfahrungsgesteuerte Selbstfähigkeitserkennung
de.summary: MACRO ist ein selbstevolutionierender medizinischer Agent, der autonome wiederverwendbare Werkzeugsequenzen aus Ausführungserfahrung entdeckt und synthetisiert und die Notwendigkeit manueller Umgestaltung bei Umgebungsveränderungen eliminiert. Das System nutzt visuell-klinisches Gedächtnis und Verstärkungslernen, um die mehrstufige medizinische Bildanalyse zu verbessern. Experimente über verschiedene Bildgebungsaufgaben zeigen konsistente Verbesserungen in Orchestrierungsgenauigkeit und domänenübergreifender Generalisierung.

es.title: Agentes de Imagería Médica en Evolución a través del Descubrimiento Autónomo de Habilidades Impulsado por la Experiencia
es.summary: MACRO es un agente médico auto-evolutivo que descubre y sintetiza autónomamente secuencias de herramientas reutilizables a partir de la experiencia de ejecución, eliminando la necesidad de rediseño manual cuando los entornos cambian. El sistema utiliza memoria visual-clínica y aprendizaje por refuerzo para mejorar el análisis de imágenes médicas de múltiples pasos. Los experimentos en diversas tareas de imagería demuestran mejoras consistentes en la precisión de orquestación y la generalización entre dominios.

title: The World Won't Stay Still: Programmable Evolution for Agent Benchmarks
summary: ProEvolve provides a graph-based framework for programmable environment evolution, enabling automatic generation of diverse benchmarks that test agent adaptability to changing tools, schemas, and data. Rather than assuming static environments, the system generates 200 evolved environments and 3,000 task sandboxes to comprehensively evaluate robustness. This addresses a critical gap in existing agent benchmarks that fail to reflect real-world dynamics.
content:
arXiv:2603.05910v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM-powered agents fulfill user requests by interacting with environments, querying data, and invoking tools in a multi-turn process. Yet, most existing benchmarks assume static environments with fixed schemas and toolsets, neglecting the evolutionary nature of the real world and agents' robustness to environmental changes. In this paper, we study a crucial problem: how to evolve the agent environment in a scalable and controllable way, thereby better evaluating agents' adaptability to real-world dynamics. We propose ProEvolve, a graph-based framework that makes environment evolution programmable. At its core, a typed relational graph provides a unified, explicit representation of the environment: data, tools, and schema. Under this formalism, adding, removing, or modifying capabilities are expressed as graph transformations that coherently propagate updates across tools, schemas, and data access. Building on this, ProEvolve can (1) program the evolutionary dynamics as graph transformations to generate environments automatically, and (2) instantiate task sandboxes via subgraph sampling and programming. We validate ProEvolve by evolving a single environment into 200 environments and 3,000 task sandboxes, and benchmark representative agents accordingly.

zh.title: 世界不会停滞：代理基准的可编程演进
zh.summary: ProEvolve提供了一个基于图的可编程环境演进框架，能够自动生成多样化基准来测试代理对工具、模式和数据变化的适应性。该系统不是假设静态环境，而是生成200个演进环境和3000个任务沙盒来全面评估鲁棒性，这解决了现有代理基准未能反映现实动态的关键差距。

fr.title: Le monde ne s'arrêtera pas : Évolution programmable pour les benchmarks des agents
fr.summary: ProEvolve fournit un cadre basé sur des graphes pour l'évolution programmable de l'environnement, permettant la génération automatique de benchmarks variés qui testent l'adaptabilité des agents aux outils, schémas et données changeants. Plutôt que de supposer des environnements statiques, le système génère 200 environnements évolutifs et 3 000 bacs à sable de tâches pour évaluer de manière exhaustive la robustesse. Ceci résout une lacune critique dans les benchmarks d'agents existants qui ne reflètent pas la dynamique du monde réel.

de.title: Die Welt wird nicht stehen bleiben: Programmierbare Evolution für Agent-Benchmarks
de.summary: ProEvolve bietet ein graph-basiertes Framework für programmierbare Umgebungsevolution und ermöglicht die automatische Generierung vielfältiger Benchmarks, die die Fähigkeit von Agenten zur Anpassung an verändernde Tools, Schemata und Daten testen. Anstatt statische Umgebungen anzunehmen, generiert das System 200 entwickelte Umgebungen und 3.000 Task-Sandboxen, um die Robustheit umfassend zu bewerten. Dies adressiert eine kritische Lücke in bestehenden Agent-Benchmarks, die die realen Dynamiken nicht widerspiegeln.

es.title: El Mundo No Se Detendrá: Evolución Programable para Benchmarks de Agentes
es.summary: ProEvolve proporciona un marco basado en grafos para la evolución programable del entorno, permitiendo la generación automática de benchmarks diversos que prueben la adaptabilidad de los agentes a herramientas, esquemas y datos cambiantes. En lugar de asumir entornos estáticos, el sistema genera 200 entornos evolucionados y 3.000 zonas de prueba de tareas para evaluar integralmente la robustez. Esto aborda una brecha crítica en los benchmarks de agentes existentes que no reflejan la dinámica del mundo real.

title: DeepFact: Co-Evolving Benchmarks and Agents for Deep Research Factuality
summary: DeepFact introduces Evolving Benchmarking via Audit-then-Score (AtS), where expert disagreements on factuality are audited and benchmarks revised accordingly, improving accuracy from 60.8% to 90.9% across four rounds. The framework creates versioned benchmarks with auditable rationales and verification agents that outperform existing fact-checkers on complex research documents. This approach enables more reliable evaluation of claim-level factuality in deep research reports.
content:
arXiv:2603.05912v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Search-augmented LLM agents can produce deep research reports (DRRs), but verifying claim-level factuality remains challenging. Existing fact-checkers are primarily designed for general-domain, factoid-style atomic claims, and there is no benchmark to test whether such verifiers transfer to DRRs. Yet building such a benchmark is itself difficult. We first show that static expert-labeled benchmarks are brittle in this setting: in a controlled study with PhD-level specialists, unassisted experts achieve only 60.8% accuracy on a hidden micro-gold set of verifiable claims. We propose Evolving Benchmarking via Audit-then-Score (AtS), where benchmark labels and rationales are explicitly revisable: when a verifier disagrees with the current benchmark, it must submit evidence; an auditor adjudicates the dispute; and accepted revisions update the benchmark before models are scored. Across four AtS rounds, expert micro-gold accuracy rises to 90.9%, indicating experts are substantially more reliable as auditors than as one-shot labelers. We instantiate AtS as DeepFact-Bench, a versioned DRR factuality benchmark with auditable rationales, and DeepFact-Eval, a document-level verification agent (with a grouped lite variant) that outperforms existing verifiers on DeepFact-Bench and transfers well to external factuality datasets.

zh.title: DeepFact：深度研究事实性的共同演进基准和代理
zh.summary: DeepFact引入了通过审计-然后-评分（AtS）的演进基准，其中对事实性的专家分歧被审计，基准相应修订，在四轮中的准确度从60.8%提高到90.9%。该框架创建了具有可审计理由的版本化基准和验证代理，在复杂研究文档上的性能超过现有事实核查员。这种方法为深度研究报告中的声明级别事实性的更可靠评估奠定了基础。

fr.title: DeepFact : Benchmarks et Agents Co-Évolutifs pour la Factualité de la Recherche Approfondie
fr.summary: DeepFact introduit l'évolution des benchmarks via Audit-then-Score (AtS), où les désaccords d'experts sur la factualité sont audités et les benchmarks révisés en conséquence, améliorant la précision de 60,8% à 90,9% sur quatre tours. Le cadre crée des benchmarks versionnés avec des justifications auditables et des agents de vérification qui surpassent les correcteurs de faits existants sur les documents de recherche complexes. Cette approche permet une évaluation plus fiable de la factualité au niveau des affirmations dans les rapports de recherche approfondie.

de.title: DeepFact: Koevolutive Benchmarks und Agenten für Tiefforschungs-Faktizität
de.summary: DeepFact führt Evolving Benchmarking mittels Audit-then-Score (AtS) ein, wobei Meinungsverschiedenheiten von Experten zur Faktizität überprüft und Benchmarks entsprechend überarbeitet werden, wobei die Genauigkeit über vier Runden von 60,8% auf 90,9% verbessert wird. Das Framework erstellt versionierte Benchmarks mit nachvollziehbaren Begründungen und Verifizierungsagenten, die bestehende Faktenprüfer bei komplexen Forschungsdokumenten übertreffen. Dieser Ansatz ermöglicht eine zuverlässigere Bewertung der Faktizität auf Aussageebene in tiefgehenden Forschungsberichten.

es.title: DeepFact: Benchmarks y Agentes Coevolutivos para la Factualidad de la Investigación Profunda
es.summary: DeepFact introduce la evolución de benchmarks mediante Auditoría-Luego-Puntuación (AtS), donde los desacuerdos de expertos sobre la factualidad se auditan y los benchmarks se revisan en consecuencia, mejorando la precisión del 60,8% al 90,9% en cuatro rondas. El marco crea benchmarks versionados con fundamentos auditables y agentes de verificación que superan a los verificadores de hechos existentes en documentos de investigación complejos. Este enfoque permite una evaluación más confiable de la factualidad a nivel de afirmación en informes de investigación profunda.

title: An Interactive Multi-Agent System for Evaluation of New Product Concepts
summary: This work proposes an automated multi-agent system using LLMs to evaluate product concepts across technical and market feasibility dimensions, with eight specialized virtual agents using retrieval-augmented generation and real-time search. Agents were fine-tuned on professional product review data to enhance judgment accuracy. Case studies demonstrate evaluation rankings consistent with senior industry experts.
content:
arXiv:2603.05980v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Product concept evaluation is a critical stage that determines strategic resource allocation and project success in enterprises. However, traditional expert-led approaches face limitations such as subjective bias and high time and cost requirements. To support this process, this study proposes an automated approach utilizing a large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent system (MAS). Through a systematic analysis of previous research on product development and team collaboration, this study established two primary evaluation dimensions, namely technical feasibility and market feasibility. The proposed system consists of a team of eight virtual agents representing specialized domains such as R&D and marketing. These agents use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and real-time search tools to gather objective evidence and validate concepts through structured deliberations based on the established criteria. The agents were further fine-tuned using professional product review data to enhance their judgment accuracy. A case study involving professional display monitor concepts demonstrated that the system's evaluation rankings were consistent with those of senior industry experts. These results confirm the usability of the proposed multi-agent-based evaluation approach for supporting product development decisions.

zh.title: 用于评估新产品概念的交互式多智能体系统
zh.summary: 这项工作提出了一个自动化的多智能体系统，利用大型语言模型来评估产品概念的技术和市场可行性维度，包含八个专门的虚拟智能体，使用检索增强生成和实时搜索。智能体在专业产品评论数据上进行了微调以增强判断准确性。案例研究表明评估排名与资深行业专家一致。

fr.title: Un système multi-agents interactif pour l'évaluation de nouveaux concepts de produits
fr.summary: Ce travail propose un système multi-agents automatisé utilisant des modèles de langage pour évaluer les concepts de produits selon les dimensions de faisabilité technique et commerciale, avec huit agents virtuels spécialisés utilisant la génération augmentée par récupération et la recherche en temps réel. Les agents ont été affinés sur des données d'avis de produits professionnels pour améliorer la précision du jugement. Les études de cas démontrent des classements d'évaluation cohérents avec les experts seniors de l'industrie.

de.title: Ein interaktives Multi-Agent-System zur Evaluierung neuer Produktkonzepte
de.summary: Diese Arbeit schlägt ein automatisiertes Multi-Agent-System vor, das große Sprachmodelle verwendet, um Produktkonzepte über technische und marktliche Machbarkeitsdimensionen zu bewerten, mit acht spezialisierten virtuellen Agenten, die Retrieval-augmentierte Generierung und Echtzeitsuche verwenden. Agenten wurden auf professionelle Produktbewertungsdaten abgestimmt, um die Urteilsgenauigkeit zu verbessern. Fallstudien zeigen Bewertungsrankings, die mit erfahrenen Branchenexperten übereinstimmen.

es.title: Un sistema multi-agente interactivo para la evaluación de nuevos conceptos de productos
es.summary: Este trabajo propone un sistema multi-agente automatizado que utiliza modelos de lenguaje grandes para evaluar conceptos de productos en dimensiones de viabilidad técnica y de mercado, con ocho agentes virtuales especializados que utilizan generación aumentada por recuperación y búsqueda en tiempo real. Los agentes se ajustaron en datos de reseñas de productos profesionales para mejorar la precisión del juicio. Los estudios de casos demuestran clasificaciones de evaluación consistentes con expertos senior de la industria.

title: Agentic LLM Planning via Step-Wise PDDL Simulation: An Empirical Characterisation
summary: PyPDDLEngine enables interactive LLM-based task planning through step-wise PDDL simulation where models select actions sequentially and observe results. Agentic planning achieves 66.7% success versus 63.7% for direct planning on blocksworld instances, though at 5.7× higher token cost. Results suggest interactive feedback helps when externally grounded (like compiler errors), unlike self-assessed environment feedback.
content:
arXiv:2603.06064v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Task planning, the problem of sequencing actions to reach a goal from an initial state, is a core capability requirement for autonomous robotic systems. Whether large language models (LLMs) can serve as viable planners alongside classical symbolic methods remains an open question. We present PyPDDLEngine, an open-source Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL) simulation engine that exposes planning operations as LLM tool calls through a Model Context Protocol (MCP) interface. Rather than committing to a complete action sequence upfront, the LLM acts as an interactive search policy that selects one action at a time, observes each resulting state, and can reset and retry. We evaluate four approaches on 102 International Planning Competition (IPC) Blocksworld instances under a uniform 180-second budget: Fast Downward lama-first and seq-sat-lama-2011 as classical baselines, direct LLM planning (Claude Haiku 4.5), and agentic LLM planning via PyPDDLEngine. Fast Downward achieves 85.3% success. The direct and agentic LLM approaches achieve 63.7% and 66.7%, respectively, a consistent but modest three-percentage-point advantage for the agentic approach at $5.7\times$ higher token cost per solution. Across most co-solved difficulty blocks, both LLM approaches produce shorter plans than seq-sat-lama-2011 despite its iterative quality improvement, a result consistent with training-data recall rather than generalisable planning. These results suggest that agentic gains depend on the nature of environmental feedback. Coding agents benefit from externally grounded signals such as compiler errors and test failures, whereas PDDL step feedback is self-assessed, leaving the agent to evaluate its own progress without external verification.

zh.title: 通过逐步PDDL模拟的智能体LLM规划：实证表征
zh.summary: PyPDDLEngine通过逐步PDDL模拟实现交互式LLM任务规划，其中模型顺序选择行动并观察结果。智能体规划在blocksworld实例上达到66.7%的成功率，相比直接规划的63.7%，尽管成本增加了5.7倍。结果表明交互式反馈在外部有根据的情况下有帮助（如编译器错误），不同于自我评估的环境反馈。

fr.title: Planification LLM agentic via simulation PDDL pas à pas : une caractérisation empirique
fr.summary: PyPDDLEngine permet la planification de tâches basée sur LLM interactive via simulation PDDL pas à pas où les modèles sélectionnent les actions séquentiellement et observent les résultats. La planification agentic atteint 66,7 % de succès contre 63,7 % pour la planification directe sur les instances blocksworld, bien qu'à un coût 5,7 × plus élevé. Les résultats suggèrent que la rétroaction interactive aide lorsqu'elle est ancrée en externe (comme les erreurs du compilateur), contrairement à la rétroaction d'environnement auto-évaluée.

de.title: Agentic LLM-Planung via schrittweise PDDL-Simulation: Eine empirische Charakterisierung
de.summary: PyPDDLEngine ermöglicht interaktive LLM-basierte Aufgabenplanung durch schrittweise PDDL-Simulation, bei der Modelle Aktionen nacheinander auswählen und Ergebnisse beobachten. Agentic Planning erreicht 66,7 % Erfolgsquote gegenüber 63,7 % bei direkter Planung auf Blocksworld-Instanzen, jedoch mit 5,7-fach höheren Token-Kosten. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass interaktive Rückmeldungen helfen, wenn sie extern fundiert sind (wie Compilerfehler), anders als selbst bewertete Umgebungsrückmeldungen.

es.title: Planificación agentic de LLM a través de simulación PDDL paso a paso: una caracterización empírica
es.summary: PyPDDLEngine permite la planificación de tareas interactiva basada en LLM mediante simulación PDDL paso a paso donde los modelos seleccionan acciones secuencialmente y observan los resultados. La planificación agentic logra 66,7 % de éxito frente a 63,7 % para planificación directa en instancias de blocksworld, aunque con un costo 5,7 × superior. Los resultados sugieren que la retroalimentación interactiva ayuda cuando está fundamentada externamente (como errores del compilador), a diferencia de la retroalimentación del entorno autoevaluada.

title: Aggregative Semantics for Quantitative Bipolar Argumentation Frameworks
summary: Aggregative semantics introduce a three-stage computation for quantitative bipolar argumentation frameworks, separately aggregating attackers and supporters before combining with intrinsic argument weights. This approach maintains bipolarity throughout the reasoning process and provides more interpretable and parametrizable gradual semantics. Testing of five hundred aggregative semantics demonstrates the range of possible behaviors.
content:
arXiv:2603.06067v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Formal argumentation is being used increasingly in artificial intelligence as an effective and understandable way to model potentially conflicting pieces of information, called arguments, and identify so-called acceptable arguments depending on a chosen semantics. This paper deals with the specific context of Quantitative Bipolar Argumentation Frameworks (QBAF), where arguments have intrinsic weights and can attack or support each other. In this context, we introduce a novel family of gradual semantics, called aggregative semantics. In order to deal with situations in which attackers and supporters do not play a symmetric role, and in contrast to modular semantics, we propose to aggregate attackers and supporters separately. This leads to a three-stage computation, which consists in computing a global weight for attackers and another for supporters, before aggregating these two values with the intrinsic weight of the argument. We discuss the properties that the three aggregation functions should satisfy depending on the context, as well as their relationships with the classical principles for gradual semantics. This discussion is supported by various simple examples, as well as a final example on which five hundred aggregative semantics are tested and compared, illustrating the range of possible behaviours with aggregative semantics. Decomposing the computation into three distinct and interpretable steps leads to a more parametrisable computation: it keeps the bipolarity one step further than what is done in the literature, and it leads to more understandable gradual semantics.

zh.title: 量化双极辩论框架的聚合语义
zh.summary: 聚合语义为量化双极辩论框架引入了三阶段计算，分别聚合攻击者和支持者，然后与内在论证权重结合。这种方法在整个推理过程中保持双极性，并提供更可解释和参数化的渐进语义。对五百种聚合语义的测试展示了可能行为的范围。

fr.title: Sémantique agrégative pour les cadres d'argumentation bipolaires quantitatifs
fr.summary: La sémantique agrégative introduit un calcul en trois étapes pour les cadres d'argumentation bipolaires quantitatifs, agrégant séparément les attaquants et les partisans avant de combiner avec les poids d'arguments intrinsèques. Cette approche maintient la bipolarity tout au long du processus de raisonnement et fournit une sémantique graduelle plus interprétable et paramétrable. Les tests de cinq cents sémantiques agrégatives démontrent la gamme de comportements possibles.

de.title: Aggregative Semantik für quantitative bipolare Argumentationsrahmen
de.summary: Aggregative Semantik führt eine dreistufige Berechnung für quantitative bipolare Argumentationsrahmen ein, wobei Angreifer und Befürworter separat aggregiert werden, bevor sie mit intrinsischen Argumentgewichten kombiniert werden. Dieser Ansatz behält die Bipolarität während des gesamten Reasoning-Prozesses bei und bietet aussagekräftigere und parametrisierbare graduelle Semantik. Tests von fünfhundert aggregativen Semantiken zeigen die Bandbreite möglicher Verhaltensweisen.

es.title: Semántica agregativa para marcos de argumentación bipolares cuantitativos
es.summary: La semántica agregativa introduce un cálculo en tres fases para marcos de argumentación bipolares cuantitativos, agregando separadamente atacantes y partidarios antes de combinar con pesos de argumentos intrínsecos. Este enfoque mantiene la bipolarity a lo largo del proceso de razonamiento y proporciona una semántica gradual más interpretable y parametrizable. Las pruebas de quinientos semánticas agregativas demuestran el rango de comportamientos posibles.

title: Offline Materials Optimization with CliqueFlowmer
summary: CliqueFlowmer combines clique-based model-based optimization with transformer and flow generation for computational materials discovery, fusing direct optimization of target properties into generation. The approach outperforms standard generative baselines in discovering materials with superior properties. Code is open-sourced to support interdisciplinary materials optimization research.
content:
arXiv:2603.06082v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent advances in deep learning inspired neural network-based approaches to computational materials discovery (CMD). A plethora of problems in this field involve finding materials that optimize a target property. Nevertheless, the increasingly popular generative modeling methods are ineffective at boldly exploring attractive regions of the materials space due to their maximum likelihood training. In this work, we offer an alternative CMD technique based on offline model-based optimization (MBO) that fuses direct optimization of a target material property into generation. To that end, we introduce a domain-specific model, dubbed CliqueFlowmer, that incorporates recent advances of clique-based MBO into transformer and flow generation. We validate CliqueFlowmer's optimization abilities and show that materials it produces strongly outperform those provided by generative baselines. To enable employment of CliqueFlowmer in specialized materials optimization problems and support interdisciplinary research, we open-source our code at https://github.com/znowu/CliqueFlowmer.

zh.title: 离线材料优化与CliqueFlowmer
zh.summary: CliqueFlowmer结合团基模型优化、变压器和流生成技术进行计算材料发现，将目标属性的直接优化融入生成过程。该方法在发现具有优异性能的材料方面优于标准生成基线。代码已开源，支持跨学科的材料优化研究。

fr.title: Optimisation des matériaux hors ligne avec CliqueFlowmer
fr.summary: CliqueFlowmer combine l'optimisation basée sur des modèles de cliques avec les transformateurs et la génération de flux pour la découverte computationnelle de matériaux, intégrant l'optimisation directe des propriétés cibles dans la génération. L'approche surpasse les lignes de base génératives standard dans la découverte de matériaux aux propriétés supérieures. Le code est open-source pour soutenir la recherche interdisciplinaire en optimisation de matériaux.

de.title: Offline-Materialoptimierung mit CliqueFlowmer
de.summary: CliqueFlowmer kombiniert kliq-basierte modellgestützte Optimierung mit Transformern und Flusserzeugung für rechnerische Materialentdeckung und integriert die direkte Optimierung von Zieleigenschaften in die Erzeugung. Der Ansatz übertrifft standardmäßige generative Baselines bei der Entdeckung von Materialien mit überlegenen Eigenschaften. Der Code ist quelloffen, um interdisziplinäre Materialoptimierungsforschung zu unterstützen.

es.title: Optimización de materiales sin conexión con CliqueFlowmer
es.summary: CliqueFlowmer combina optimización basada en modelos de cliques con transformadores y generación de flujo para el descubrimiento computacional de materiales, integrando la optimización directa de propiedades objetivo en la generación. El enfoque supera las líneas base generativas estándar en el descubrimiento de materiales con propiedades superiores. El código es de código abierto para apoyar la investigación interdisciplinaria en optimización de materiales.

title: Conversational Demand Response: Bidirectional Aggregator-Prosumer Coordination through Agentic AI
summary: Conversational Demand Response enables bidirectional natural language coordination between energy aggregators and residential prosumers through agentic AI, completing interactions in under 12 seconds. The two-tier architecture allows prosumers to assess deliverability and cost-benefit through optimization tools while enabling upstream communication of preference changes. This bridges automated efficiency with user agency for sustained energy participation.
content:
arXiv:2603.06217v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Residential demand response depends on sustained prosumer participation, yet existing coordination is either fully automated, or limited to one-way dispatch signals and price alerts that offer little possibility for informed decision-making. This paper introduces Conversational Demand Response (CDR), a coordination mechanism where aggregators and prosumers interact through bidirectional natural language, enabled through agentic AI. A two-tier multi-agent architecture is developed in which an aggregator agent dispatches flexibility requests and a prosumer Home Energy Management System (HEMS) assesses deliverability and cost-benefit by calling an optimization-based tool. CDR also enables prosumer-initiated upstream communication, where changes in preferences can reach the aggregator directly. Proof-of-concept evaluation shows that interactions complete in under 12 seconds. The architecture illustrates how agentic AI can bridge the aggregator-prosumer coordination gap, providing the scalability of automated DR while preserving the transparency, explainability, and user agency necessary for sustained prosumer participation. All system components, including agent prompts, orchestration logic, and simulation interfaces, are released as open source to enable reproducibility and further development.

zh.title: 会话式需求响应：通过智能体AI的双向聚合器-消费者协调
zh.summary: 会话式需求响应通过智能体AI实现能源聚合器和住宅消费者之间的双向自然语言协调，交互在12秒内完成。双层架构允许消费者通过优化工具评估可交付性和成本效益，同时支持上游传达偏好变化。这在自动化效率和用户权限之间取得平衡，以实现持续的能源参与。

fr.title: Réponse à la demande conversationnelle : Coordination bidirectionnelle agrégateur-prosommateur par IA agentique
fr.summary: La réponse à la demande conversationnelle permet la coordination bidirectionnelle en langage naturel entre les agrégateurs d'énergie et les prosommateurs résidentiels par IA agentique, complétant les interactions en moins de 12 secondes. L'architecture à deux niveaux permet aux prosommateurs d'évaluer la faisabilité et le rapport coût-bénéfice via des outils d'optimisation tout en permettant la communication en amont des changements de préférences. Ceci rapproche l'efficacité automatisée et l'agentivité de l'utilisateur pour une participation énergétique soutenue.

de.title: Gesprächsgesteuerte Nachfrageregelung: Bidirektionale Aggregator-Prosumenten-Koordination durch intelligente KI
de.summary: Die gesprächsgesteuerte Nachfrageregelung ermöglicht bidirektionale Koordination in natürlicher Sprache zwischen Energieaggregatoren und Wohnprosumenten durch intelligente KI, wobei Wechselwirkungen in weniger als 12 Sekunden abgeschlossen werden. Die zweistufige Architektur ermöglicht es Prosumenten, Lieferfähigkeit und Kosten-Nutzen-Verhältnis durch Optimierungswerkzeuge zu bewerten und gleichzeitig die Kommunikation von Präferenzänderungen upstream zu ermöglichen. Dies verbindet automatisierte Effizienz mit Nutzerautonomie für anhaltende Energiebeteiligung.

es.title: Respuesta de demanda conversacional: Coordinación bidireccional agregador-prosumidor a través de IA agente
es.summary: La respuesta de demanda conversacional permite coordinación bidireccional en lenguaje natural entre agregadores de energía y prosumidores residenciales a través de IA agente, completando interacciones en menos de 12 segundos. La arquitectura de dos niveles permite que los prosumidores evalúen la viabilidad y la relación costo-beneficio a través de herramientas de optimización mientras permiten la comunicación ascendente de cambios de preferencia. Esto cierra la brecha entre la eficiencia automatizada y la agencia del usuario para la participación energética sostenida.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Climate Adaptation: Reinforcement Learning for Climate Change-Resilient Transport
summary: This framework applies reinforcement learning to long-term flood adaptation planning for urban transport, combining rainfall/flood modeling, transport simulation, and impact quantification. RL discovers coordinated spatial-temporal adaptation strategies that balance investment costs against avoided flood impacts, outperforming traditional optimization. Results demonstrate practical decision-support for climate-resilient infrastructure over the 2024-2100 period.
content:
arXiv:2603.06278v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Climate change is expected to intensify rainfall and, consequently, pluvial flooding, leading to increased disruptions in urban transportation systems over the coming decades. Designing effective adaptation strategies is challenging due to the long-term, sequential nature of infrastructure investments, deep climate uncertainty, and the complex interactions between flooding, infrastructure, and mobility impacts. In this work, we propose a novel decision-support framework using reinforcement learning (RL) for long-term flood adaptation planning. Formulated as an integrated assessment model (IAM), the framework combines rainfall projection and flood modeling, transport simulation, and quantification of direct and indirect impacts on infrastructure and mobility. Our RL-based approach learns adaptive strategies that balance investment and maintenance costs against avoided impacts. We evaluate the framework through a case study of Copenhagen's inner city over the 2024-2100 period, testing multiple adaptation options, and different belief and realized climate scenarios. Results show that the framework outperforms traditional optimization approaches by discovering coordinated spatial and temporal adaptation pathways and learning trade-offs between impact reduction and adaptation investment, yielding more resilient strategies. Overall, our results showcase the potential of reinforcement learning as a flexible decision-support tool for adaptive infrastructure planning under climate uncertainty.

zh.title: 气候适应的人工智能：气候变化适应性运输的强化学习
zh.summary: 该框架将强化学习应用于城市运输的长期洪水适应规划，结合降雨/洪水建模、运输模拟和影响量化。强化学习发现平衡投资成本与避免洪水影响的协调时空适应策略，优于传统优化方法。结果展示了2024-2100年间气候适应性基础设施的实际决策支持。

fr.title: Intelligence artificielle pour l'adaptation climatique : Apprentissage par renforcement pour les transports résilients au changement climatique
fr.summary: Ce cadre applique l'apprentissage par renforcement à la planification d'adaptation aux inondations à long terme pour les transports urbains, combinant la modélisation des précipitations/inondations, la simulation des transports et la quantification des impacts. L'apprentissage par renforcement découvre des stratégies d'adaptation spatio-temporelles coordonnées qui équilibrent les coûts d'investissement par rapport aux impacts d'inondation évités, surpassant l'optimisation traditionnelle. Les résultats démontrent un soutien décisionnel pratique pour une infrastructure résiliente au changement climatique au cours de la période 2024-2100.

de.title: Künstliche Intelligenz zur Klimaanpassung: Verstärkungslernen für klimawandelresistente Verkehrssysteme
de.summary: Dieses Framework wendet Verstärkungslernen auf die Langzeit-Hochwasseradaptationsplanung für städtische Verkehrssysteme an und kombiniert Niederschlags-/Hochwassermodellierung, Verkehrssimulation und Auswirkungsquantifizierung. Verstärkungslernen entdeckt koordinierte räumlich-zeitliche Adaptationsstrategien, die Investitionskosten gegen vermiedene Hochwasserauswirkungen abwägen und traditionelle Optimierung übertreffen. Die Ergebnisse zeigen praktische Entscheidungsunterstützung für klimawandelresistente Infrastruktur für den Zeitraum 2024-2100.

es.title: Inteligencia artificial para la adaptación al cambio climático: Aprendizaje por refuerzo para el transporte resiliente al cambio climático
es.summary: Este marco aplica aprendizaje por refuerzo a la planificación de adaptación a inundaciones a largo plazo para el transporte urbano, combinando modelado de lluvia/inundaciones, simulación de transporte y cuantificación de impactos. El aprendizaje por refuerzo descubre estrategias de adaptación espaciotemporal coordinadas que equilibran los costos de inversión frente a los impactos de inundación evitados, superando la optimización tradicional. Los resultados demuestran apoyo decisional práctico para infraestructura resiliente al clima durante el período 2024-2100.

title: The EpisTwin: A Knowledge Graph-Grounded Neuro-Symbolic Architecture for Personal AI
summary: EpisTwin grounds personal AI in a user-centric Personal Knowledge Graph, using multimodal language models to integrate fragmented user data into semantic triples. The neuro-symbolic architecture combines graph retrieval with visual refinement for holistic sensemaking while maintaining verifiable, structured reasoning. This addresses limitations of vector-based retrieval in capturing semantic topology and temporal dependencies.
content:
arXiv:2603.06290v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Personal Artificial Intelligence is currently hindered by the fragmentation of user data across isolated silos. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation offers a partial remedy, its reliance on unstructured vector similarity fails to capture the latent semantic topology and temporal dependencies essential for holistic sensemaking. We introduce EpisTwin, a neuro-symbolic framework that grounds generative reasoning in a verifiable, user-centric Personal Knowledge Graph. EpisTwin leverages Multimodal Language Models to lift heterogeneous, cross-application data into semantic triples. At inference, EpisTwin enables complex reasoning over the personal semantic graph via an agentic coordinator that combines Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Online Deep Visual Refinement, dynamically re-grounding symbolic entities in their raw visual context. We also introduce PersonalQA-71-100, a synthetic benchmark designed to simulate a realistic user's digital footprint and evaluate EpisTwin performance. Our framework demonstrates robust results across a suite of state-of-the-art judge models, offering a promising direction for trustworthy Personal AI.

zh.title: EpisTwin：用于个人人工智能的知识图谱基础神经符号架构
zh.summary: EpisTwin将个人人工智能根植于以用户为中心的个人知识图谱，使用多模态语言模型将碎片化的用户数据集成为语义三元组。神经符号架构结合了图检索与视觉精化，实现整体意义理解，同时保持可验证的结构化推理。这解决了基于向量的检索在捕捉语义拓扑和时间依赖关系方面的局限性。

fr.title: EpisTwin : une architecture neuro-symbolique ancrée dans un graphique de connaissances pour l'IA personnelle
fr.summary: EpisTwin ancre l'IA personnelle dans un Graphique de Connaissances Personnel centré sur l'utilisateur, en utilisant des modèles de langage multimodaux pour intégrer les données utilisateur fragmentées en triples sémantiques. L'architecture neuro-symbolique combine la récupération de graphiques avec l'affinement visuel pour une compréhension holistique tout en maintenant un raisonnement structuré et vérifiable. Cela résout les limitations de la récupération basée sur les vecteurs dans la capture de la topologie sémantique et des dépendances temporelles.

de.title: EpisTwin: Eine wissensgrafikgestützte neurosymbolische Architektur für persönliche KI
de.summary: EpisTwin verankert persönliche KI in einem benutzerorientierten persönlichen Wissensgraph, wobei multimodale Sprachmodelle fragmentierte Benutzerdaten in semantische Tripel integrieren. Die neurosymbolische Architektur kombiniert Graphabruf mit visueller Verfeinerung für ganzheitliches Sinnerkennen, während überprüfbare strukturierte Argumentation erhalten bleibt. Dies löst Beschränkungen des vektorbasierten Abrufs bei der Erfassung semantischer Topologie und zeitlicher Abhängigkeiten.

es.title: EpisTwin: Una arquitectura neuro-simbólica fundamentada en gráficos de conocimiento para IA personal
es.summary: EpisTwin fundamenta la IA personal en un Gráfico de Conocimiento Personal centrado en el usuario, utilizando modelos de lenguaje multimodal para integrar datos de usuario fragmentados en triples semánticos. La arquitectura neuro-simbólica combina recuperación de gráficos con refinamiento visual para la comprensión holística mientras mantiene un razonamiento estructurado y verificable. Esto aborda las limitaciones de la recuperación basada en vectores para capturar la topología semántica y las dependencias temporales.

title: SAHOO: Safeguarded Alignment for High-Order Optimization Objectives in Recursive Self-Improvement
summary: SAHOO monitors and controls alignment drift during recursive self-improvement using three safeguards: Goal Drift Index for semantic drift detection, constraint preservation checks for safety-critical invariants, and regression-risk quantification. The framework achieves 18.3% quality improvement in code while maintaining constraints, and maps the capability-alignment frontier. Results demonstrate measurable alignment preservation at scale.
content:
arXiv:2603.06333v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recursive self-improvement is moving from theory to practice: modern systems can critique, revise, and evaluate their own outputs, yet iterative self-modification risks subtle alignment drift. We introduce SAHOO, a practical framework to monitor and control drift through three safeguards: (i) the Goal Drift Index (GDI), a learned multi-signal detector combining semantic, lexical, structural, and distributional measures; (ii) constraint preservation checks that enforce safety-critical invariants such as syntactic correctness and non-hallucination; and (iii) regression-risk quantification to flag improvement cycles that undo prior gains. Across 189 tasks in code generation, mathematical reasoning, and truthfulness, SAHOO produces substantial quality gains, including 18.3 percent improvement in code tasks and 16.8 percent in reasoning, while preserving constraints in two domains and maintaining low violations in truthfulness. Thresholds are calibrated on a small validation set of 18 tasks across three cycles. We further map the capability-alignment frontier, showing efficient early improvement cycles but rising alignment costs later and exposing domain-specific tensions such as fluency versus factuality. SAHOO therefore makes alignment preservation during recursive self-improvement measurable, deployable, and systematically validated at scale.

zh.title: SAHOO：递归自我改进中高阶优化目标的保护性对齐
zh.summary: SAHOO使用三个保护措施监控和控制递归自我改进期间的对齐漂移：用于语义漂移检测的目标漂移指数、用于安全关键不变量的约束保留检查，以及回归风险量化。该框架在保持约束的同时实现了代码质量18.3%的改进，并映射了能力-对齐前沿。结果证明了大规模可测量的对齐保留。

fr.title: SAHOO : Alignement sauvegardé pour les objectifs d'optimisation d'ordre élevé dans l'auto-amélioration récursive
fr.summary: SAHOO surveille et contrôle la dérive d'alignement lors de l'auto-amélioration récursive en utilisant trois mécanismes de protection : l'Indice de Dérive d'Objectif pour la détection de dérive sémantique, les vérifications de préservation de contraintes pour les invariants critiques de sécurité, et la quantification du risque de régression. Le cadre réalise une amélioration de la qualité de 18,3% dans le code tout en maintenant les contraintes, et trace la frontière capacité-alignement. Les résultats démontrent une préservation d'alignement mesurable à l'échelle.

de.title: SAHOO: Geschützte Ausrichtung für hochordentliche Optimierungsziele bei rekursiver Selbstverbesserung
de.summary: SAHOO überwacht und kontrolliert die Ausrichtungsabweichung während der rekursiven Selbstverbesserung mithilfe von drei Schutzmaßnahmen: Zieldrift-Index zur Erkennung semantischer Abweichungen, Constraint-Erhaltungsprüfungen für sicherheitskritische Invarianten und RegressionsrisikoQuantifizierung. Das Framework erreicht eine Qualitätsverbesserung von 18,3% im Code und behält dabei Einschränkungen bei, und ordnet die Fähigkeits-Ausrichtungs-Grenze. Die Ergebnisse zeigen eine messbare Ausrichtungserhaltung im großen Maßstab.

es.title: SAHOO: Alineación salvaguardada para objetivos de optimización de alto orden en automejoría recursiva
es.summary: SAHOO monitorea y controla la desviación de alineación durante la automejoría recursiva utilizando tres salvaguardas: Índice de Desviación de Objetivo para la detección de desviación semántica, verificaciones de preservación de restricciones para invariantes críticos de seguridad, y cuantificación de riesgo de regresión. El marco logra una mejora de calidad del 18,3% en código mientras mantiene restricciones, y mapea la frontera capacidad-alineación. Los resultados demuestran la preservación de alineación medible a escala.

title: Talk Freely, Execute Strictly: Schema-Gated Agentic AI for Flexible and Reproducible Scientific Workflows
summary: Schema-gated orchestration separates conversational flexibility from execution authority, ensuring scientific workflows validate against machine-checkable specifications before running. This resolves the tension between rigid automation and uncontrolled flexibility through mandatory schema validation at the composed-workflow level. Multi-model LLM evaluation confirms this architecture can decouple the determinism-flexibility trade-off in scientific computing.
content:
arXiv:2603.06394v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can now translate a researcher's plain-language goal into executable computation, yet scientific workflows demand determinism, provenance, and governance that are difficult to guarantee when an LLM decides what runs. Semi-structured interviews with 18 experts across 10 industrial R&D stakeholders surface 2 competing requirements--deterministic, constrained execution and conversational flexibility without workflow rigidity--together with boundary properties (human-in-the-loop control and transparency) that any resolution must satisfy. We propose schema-gated orchestration as the resolving principle: the schema becomes a mandatory execution boundary at the composed-workflow level, so that nothing runs unless the complete action--including cross-step dependencies--validates against a machine-checkable specification.
  We operationalize the 2 requirements as execution determinism (ED) and conversational flexibility (CF), and use these axes to review 20 systems spanning 5 architectural groups along a validation-scope spectrum. Scores are assigned via a multi-model protocol--15 independent sessions across 3 LLM families--yielding substantial-to-near-perfect inter-model agreement (Krippendorff a=0.80 for ED and a=0.98 for CF), demonstrating that multi-model LLM scoring can serve as a reusable alternative to human expert panels for architectural assessment.
  The resulting landscape reveals an empirical Pareto front--no reviewed system achieves both high flexibility and high determinism--but a convergence zone emerges between the generative and workflow-centric extremes. We argue that a schema-gated architecture, separating conversational from execution authority, is positioned to decouple this trade-off, and distill 3 operational principles--clarification-before-execution, constrained plan-act orchestration, and tool-to-workflow-level gating--to guide adoption.

zh.title: 自由交谈，严格执行：用于灵活和可重现科学工作流的模式门控代理人工智能
zh.summary: 模式门控编排将对话灵活性与执行权限分离，确保科学工作流在运行前针对机器可检查的规范进行验证。这通过在组合工作流级别的强制模式验证解决了刚性自动化和不受控制的灵活性之间的张力。多模型LLM评估确认该架构可以解耦科学计算中的确定性-灵活性权衡。

fr.title: Parlez librement, exécutez strictement : IA agentique basée sur des schémas pour des flux de travail scientifiques flexibles et reproductibles
fr.summary: L'orchestration basée sur des schémas sépare la flexibilité conversationnelle de l'autorité d'exécution, garantissant que les flux de travail scientifiques se valident par rapport aux spécifications vérifiables par machine avant de s'exécuter. Cela résout la tension entre l'automatisation rigide et la flexibilité incontrôlée par la validation obligatoire des schémas au niveau du flux de travail composé. L'évaluation multi-modèle LLM confirme que cette architecture peut découpler le compromis déterminisme-flexibilité en informatique scientifique.

de.title: Sprechen Sie frei, führen Sie streng aus: Schema-gesteuerte agentische KI für flexible und reproduzierbare wissenschaftliche Arbeitsabläufe
de.summary: Die schemagesteuerte Orchestrierung trennt gesprächliche Flexibilität von Ausführungsbefugnis und stellt sicher, dass wissenschaftliche Arbeitsabläufe vor dem Ausführen gegen maschinenprüfbare Spezifikationen validiert werden. Dies löst die Spannung zwischen starrer Automatisierung und unkontrollierter Flexibilität durch verbindliche Schemavalidierung auf Ebene des zusammengesetzten Arbeitsablaufs. Die Multi-Modell-LLM-Bewertung bestätigt, dass diese Architektur die Determinismus-Flexibilität-Kompromisse in der wissenschaftlichen Informatik entkoppeln kann.

es.title: Hable libremente, ejecute estrictamente: IA agentiva basada en esquemas para flujos de trabajo científicos flexibles y reproducibles
es.summary: La orquestación basada en esquemas separa la flexibilidad conversacional de la autoridad de ejecución, asegurando que los flujos de trabajo científicos se validen con especificaciones verificables por máquina antes de ejecutarse. Esto resuelve la tensión entre la automatización rígida y la flexibilidad incontrolada mediante la validación obligatoria del esquema a nivel de flujo de trabajo compuesto. La evaluación multi-modelo LLM confirma que esta arquitectura puede desacoplar el compromiso determinismo-flexibilidad en la informática científica.
